message:
feat(doctor): add --repair flag to rebuild DB from JSONL and harden import pipeline

The doctor command now accepts `--repair` which, when errors are detected,
removes the corrupt SQLite DB (plus WAL/SHM) and rebuilds it by importing
all records from the JSONL source of truth. FK constraints are temporarily
disabled during bulk import to avoid ordering issues, then re-enabled with
an FK integrity check that cleans orphaned rows in child tables.

Key changes:
- Add DoctorArgs struct with `--repair` flag to CLI
- Doctor execute() now accepts DoctorArgs parameter
- Add SqliteStorage::execute_raw(), execute_raw_query(), execute_raw_count()
  helper methods for PRAGMA and DDL operations
- Replace recursive CTE cycle detection with iterative BFS in check_cycle()
  to avoid frankensqlite false positives on >20 rows (see #131)
- Disable FK constraints during import_from_jsonl() Phase 3 to allow
  out-of-order issue references, then clean orphaned FK rows afterward
- Normalize config keys (hyphens → underscores) in ConfigLayer::merge_from()
  and insert_key_value() so YAML keys like `issue-prefix` and `issue_prefix`
  are treated as the same canonical key
- Update tests to use normalized key names (no_db, no_daemon, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -79,7 +79,7 @@ fn main() {
             commands::blocked::execute(&args, cli.json || args.robot, &overrides, &output_ctx)
         }
         Commands::Sync(args) => commands::sync::execute(&args, cli.json, &overrides, &output_ctx),
-        Commands::Doctor => commands::doctor::execute(&overrides, &output_ctx),
+        Commands::Doctor(args) => commands::doctor::execute(&args, &overrides, &output_ctx),
         Commands::Info(args) => commands::info::execute(&args, &overrides, &output_ctx),
         Commands::Schema(args) => commands::schema::execute(&args, &overrides, &output_ctx),
         Commands::Where => commands::r#where::execute(&overrides, &output_ctx),
@@ -194,7 +194,7 @@ const fn should_auto_import(cmd: &Commands) -> bool {
         // Explicitly excluded: init, sync, diagnostic, and config commands
         Commands::Init { .. }
         | Commands::Sync(_)
-        | Commands::Doctor
+        | Commands::Doctor(_)
         | Commands::Info(_)
         | Commands::Schema(_)
         | Commands::Where
